title: Verilator seemingly using more than one thread even with `--threads 1`
body:
I suspect I'm invoking Verilator incorrectly here. I'm Verilating two designs, and then compiling the results together with a testbench and then running.

Here's my Makefile:
```Makefile
.PHONY: build

VERILATOR=verilator

simulate: /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/testbench /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/testbench_inputs.txt
	/home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/testbench < /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/testbench_inputs.txt

/home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/ground_truth_module/Vground_truth_module__ALL.a: /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/ground_truth_module/Vground_truth_module.mk

/home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/test_module/Vtest_module__ALL.a: /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/test_module/Vtest_module.mk

/home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/testbench: /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/lakeroad_result.sv /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/robustness-testing-verilog-files/generated/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit.sv /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/testbench.cc
	mkdir -p /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/test_module
	mkdir -p /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/ground_truth_module
	$(VERILATOR) --threads 1 --no-timing --Mdir /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/test_module --cc /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/lakeroad_result.sv --prefix Vtest_module -I/home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/lakeroad-private/DSP48E2 -DXIL_XECLIB -Wno-UNOPTFLAT -Wno-LATCH -Wno-WIDTH -Wno-STMTDLY -Wno-CASEX -Wno-TIMESCALEMOD -Wno-PINMISSING
	$(VERILATOR) --threads 1 --no-timing --Mdir /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/ground_truth_module --cc /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/robustness-testing-verilog-files/generated/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit.sv --prefix Vground_truth_module -I/home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/lakeroad-private/DSP48E2 -DXIL_XECLIB -Wno-UNOPTFLAT -Wno-LATCH -Wno-WIDTH -Wno-STMTDLY -Wno-CASEX -Wno-TIMESCALEMOD -Wno-PINMISSING
	make -C /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/test_module -f Vtest_module.mk
	make -C /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/ground_truth_module -f Vground_truth_module.mk
	$(CXX) -o /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/testbench -I/home/gus/verilator/include /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/testbench.cc /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/ground_truth_module/Vground_truth_module__ALL.a /home/gus/lakeroad-evaluation/out/robustness_experiments/mult_0_stage_signed_8_bit/lakeroad_xilinx_ultrascale_plus/verilator/verilator_obj_dir/test_module/Vtest_module__ALL.a /home/gus/verilator/include/verilated.cpp /home/gus/verilator/include/verilated_threads.cpp -lpthread
```

Sorry it's messy; it's automatically generated.

When I run the resulting testbench, I see it hovers at about 120% CPU, implying it's using more than one thread (despite `--thread 1`).

The testbench also runs MUCH slower than it should overall -- it's doing about 250 simulations of a very small design, but taking seconds (up to a minute) to run.

Furthermore, I looked at the flamegraph of what was happening:
![flamegraph](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/assets/1203329/0ee35de8-a8a1-45f3-a814-1dbb2a129952)

We can see most of the time is spent in threading constructs.

Am I calling Verilator incorrectly here? Why is it using threads?

Thanks so much!
